title: Modelling Accrual of Arguments in ASPIC+
abstract: This paper proposes a new formal model of argument accrual in ASPIC+ that avoids the weaknesses of earlier proposals, especially exponential blow-up, awkward premise labelling, and problems with strict arguments. It defines accrual sets, a labelling-relative defeat relation, and a fixpoint semantics, then shows that the model preserves standard argumentation semantics and applies to legal reasoning, legislative balancing, factor-based reasoning, and the linked/convergent/cumulative distinction.
